concerns.text: T013 (Generate simulated FASTQ) is marked [X] (complete) but is listed as a prerequisite for T014 (Align/Call). T014 explicitly states it must accept input from 'data/interim/synthetic_*.fastq (T013)'. If T013 is incomplete (as indicated by the 'Analyze Report' noting missing FASTQ files), T014 cannot execute its synthetic path, violating the producer-before-consumer dependency flow.

T024 is marked [X] (complete) but its description states '(Deprecated logic moved to T043)'. A task marked complete that performs no action and is explicitly deprecated creates a false dependency signal. If T024 is a consumer of T022 (FDR output), marking it complete implies the logic is done, yet the logic is actually in T043. This breaks the semantic dependency chain for the FDR output artifact.

T045 (Power-aware sweep) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on T005 (Power Analysis) and T021 (Threshold Sensitivity). T045 re-runs T005 and halts if power is insufficient. This creates a hidden sequential dependency on T005's output and T021's execution context. It cannot run in parallel with T021 or T005 without risking race conditions on the power analysis result or redundant execution.

T046 (Collinearity Guard) is marked [P] but depends on T031 (Collinearity Diagnostics) output. T031 generates `data/processed/collinearity_report.tsv`. T046 parses this file. A task that reads an artifact generated by another task cannot be parallel-safe with that producer task. The [P] tag is incorrect.

T042 (Refactor for parallel processing) depends on T041 (Profile pipeline). T041 generates `data/processed/profile_report.txt`. T042 reads this report to decide on refactoring. Marking T042 as [P] is incorrect as it requires the artifact from T041 to exist and be analyzed.
